pi_items.source: Task description explicitly specifies: 'Save the flipchart as /home/ga/Documents/Flipcharts/probability_tree_diagram.flipchart'. Verified against ActivInspire file format standards (FLIPCHART is the standard extension for Promethean ActivInspire projects).
Task description explicitly includes this directory in the full save path. Verified by examining setup_task.sh which creates this directory: 'mkdir -p /home/ga/Documents/Flipcharts'.

Task description explicitly states: 'Save the flipchart as /home/ga/Documents/Flipcharts/probability_tree_diagram.flipchart'. Verified by examining task description line 5 and export_result.sh which checks for this exact path.
Task description explicitly requires: 'Create a 3-page math lesson flipchart' with three distinct pages: Page 1 (Introduction), Page 2 (Tree Diagram), Page 3 (Practice Problems).

All terms are explicitly required in the task description: (1) 'Compound Probability' - Page 1 title, (2) 'Tree Diagram', 'Outcome', 'Probability' - Page 1 key terms, (3) 'Heads', 'Tails' - Page 2 coin flip branch labels, (4) 'HH', 'HT', 'TH', 'TT' - Page 2 final outcome labels, (5) '1/2' - probability for each coin flip, (6) '1/4' - probability for final outcomes.

A binary tree diagram for two sequential coin flips requires exactly 6 connector lines: (1) one from root to 'Heads' first flip, (2) one from root to 'Tails' first flip, (3) one from first 'Heads' to second 'Heads', (4) one from first 'Heads' to second 'Tails', (5) one from first 'Tails' to second 'Heads', (6) one from first 'Tails' to second 'Tails'. Task explicitly requires: 'Draw a tree diagram representing flipping a fair coin twice' with proper branching using 'Connector or Shape tools'.

privileged_info_summary: The probability_tree_diagram task is a synthetic interactive education task requiring creation of a 3-page ActivInspire flipchart file saved as '/home/ga/Documents/Flipcharts/probability_tree_diagram.flipchart'. Page 1 must contain the title 'Compound Probability' and the key terms 'Tree Diagram', 'Outcome', and 'Probability'. Page 2 must show a probability tree diagram for fair coin flips with 'Heads' and 'Tails' labels at all branches, final outcomes labeled 'HH', 'HT', 'TH', 'TT', and probabilities of '1/2' for primary branches and '1/4' for outcomes. Page 3 must contain a 'Practice Problems' section. The diagram requires a minimum of 6 connector lines representing the tree structure. ActivInspire flipchart files are Promethean-format projects saved as ZIP archives containing XML content.
